paper_title: Characterization of Protease P57 from Photobacterium sp. A5-7

methods.description: Procedure for determining the phylogenetic position of strain A5-7 based on its 16S rRNA gene sequence, including DNA extraction, PCR amplification, sequencing, sequence comparison, alignment, and phylogenetic tree construction.

steps.description: Genomic DNA of strain A5-7 was extracted using a bacterial genomic DNA isolation kit (BioTeke, China).
steps.searchable_summary: extract bacterial genomic DNA

steps.description: Using the genomic DNA as template, the 16S rRNA gene of strain A5-7 was amplified by PCR with primers 27F and 1492R (Lane, 1991).
steps.searchable_summary: amplify 16S rRNA gene by PCR

steps.description: The 16S rRNA gene sequence of strain A5-7 ... was sequenced at Shanghai Biosune Biotechnology Corp. (China).
steps.searchable_summary: sequence 16S rRNA gene PCR product
outputs: 16S rRNA gene sequence of strain A5-7

steps.description: The 16S rRNA gene sequence of strain A5-7, deposited in GenBank database under the accession number JX134463, was compared with those in GenBank and EzTaxon 1 (Kim et al., 2012) databases using BLASTN (Altschul et al., 1997) to determine the approximate phylogenetic affiliation and select reference sequences of related species for subsequent phylogenetic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: compare DNA sequence to databases using BLASTN and select related sequences
inputs: 16S rRNA gene sequence of strain A5-7
outputs: Reference 16S rRNA gene sequences of related species

steps.description: The 16S rRNA gene sequence of strain A5-7 was aligned with those of type strains of closely related species using the Clustal W program (Thompson et al., 1994) in the MEGA 5 (Tamura et al., 2011).
steps.searchable_summary: align multiple DNA sequences using Clustal W
inputs: 16S rRNA gene sequence of strain A5-7
Reference 16S rRNA gene sequences of related species

steps.description: The alignment obtained was manually trimmed to remove the uneven 5 and 3 ends.
steps.searchable_summary: manually trim sequence alignment ends

steps.description: Phylogenetic trees were constructed using the neighbor-joining (Saitou and Nei, 1987) and maximum-likelihood (Felsenstein, 1981) methods with MEGA 5 (Tamura et al., 2011).
steps.searchable_summary: construct phylogenetic trees using neighbor-joining and maximum-likelihood methods

steps.description: Bootstrap analyses (1000 replications) were performed to evaluate tree topologies.
steps.searchable_summary: perform bootstrap analysis for phylogenetic tree reliability
outputs: Bootstrap support values for tree branches

steps.description: Evolutionary distances were calculated using the model of Jukes and Cantor (1969).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate evolutionary distances using Jukes and Cantor model

methods.description: Procedure for purifying the protease P57 secreted by strain A5-7 from culture supernatant using centrifugation, concentration, dialysis, and anion exchange chromatography.

steps.description: Strain A5-7 was cultivated at 15 • C for 72 h in a marine LB medium supplemented with 0.3% (w/v) casein and 0.5% (w/v) gelatin in a rotatory shaker at 180 rpm.
steps.searchable_summary: cultivate bacterial strain A5-7 in specific medium

steps.description: The culture was centrifuged at 12,000 g and 4 • C for 15 min.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge bacterial culture to obtain supernatant

steps.description: The cell-free supernatant was concentrated against polyethylene glycol (PEG) 20,000.
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate protein solution using PEG

steps.description: The sample was ... then dialyzed against 20 mM phosphate buffer (pH 7.0).
steps.searchable_summary: dialyze protein sample against buffer

steps.description: The sample was loaded onto a DEAE-Sepharose Fast Flow column (GE Healthcare, USA) equilibrated with the same buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: load protein sample onto anion exchange chromatography column

steps.description: Bound proteins were eluted with a linear gradient of 0-1 M NaCl in 20 mM phosphate buffer (pH 7.0).
steps.searchable_summary: elute proteins from anion exchange column using NaCl gradient

steps.description: The protease activity of every fraction (4 mL) was measured with casein as substrate.
steps.searchable_summary: measure protease activity in chromatography fractions
outputs: Protease activity data for each fraction

steps.description: Fractions having protease activity were further subjected to 12.5% SDS-PAGE.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze active fractions by SDS-PAGE

steps.description: Then, fractions having protease activity and displaying a single band in SDS-PAGE gel were collected for further use. The purified protease was named P57.
steps.searchable_summary: collect purified protein fractions based on activity and SDS-PAGE purity

steps.description: Its N-terminal amino acid sequence was determined by the Edman degradation method with a PROCISE491 sequencer (Applied Biosystems, USA) at Peking University, China.
steps.searchable_summary: determine protein N-terminal amino acid sequence by Edman degradation
outputs: N-terminal amino acid sequence of P57

steps.description: The obtained N-terminal sequence of P57 was aligned with those of proteases in GenBank using BLASTP to determine the protease type.
steps.searchable_summary: compare protein N-terminal sequence to database using BLASTP
inputs: N-terminal amino acid sequence of P57

methods.method_name: Gene Cloning and Sequence Analysis of P57
methods.description: Procedure for cloning the gene encoding P57 and analyzing its sequence and predicted protein features, including primer design, PCR (including TAIL-PCR), sequencing, sequence assembly, domain analysis, homology search, and prediction of protein features.

steps.description: Based on the N-terminal amino acid sequence of P57 and the conserved sequence of the catalytic center of serine proteases (Maciver et al., 1994), two degenerated primers (P57N and RM6) were designed (Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: design degenerate PCR primers based on protein sequence and conserved motifs
inputs: N-terminal amino acid sequence of P57

steps.description: With the degenerated primers and the genomic DNA of strain A5-7 as template, a part of the gene encoding P57 was amplified by PCR.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify gene fragment by PCR using degenerate primers

steps.description: ...and sequenced at Shanghai Biosune Biotechnology Corp. (China).
steps.searchable_summary: sequence PCR product

steps.description: By using specific primers and general primers (Table 1), the neighboring sequences of the obtained gene fragment were amplified by thermal asymmetric interlaced PCR (TAIL-PCR) (Liu and Whittier, 1995).
steps.searchable_summary: amplify gene flanking regions using TAIL-PCR

steps.description: Through assembly, an entire gene sequence containing an ATG start codon and a TAA stop codon was obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble gene sequence from fragments

steps.description: Two specific primers (A5-7N and A5-7C) were designed ( Table 1) according to this ORF...
steps.searchable_summary: design specific PCR primers for full ORF

steps.description: ...and the full gene encoding P57 was amplified by PCR from the genomic DNA of strain A5-7...
steps.searchable_summary: amplify full gene by PCR using specific primers

steps.description: ...and verified by sequencing.
steps.searchable_summary: verify gene sequence by sequencing
outputs: Verified full gene sequence of P57

steps.description: The domain architectures of P57 were analyzed by the Conserved Domain Database (CDD) of NCBI 2 (Marchlerbauer et al., 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze protein domain architecture using CDD
inputs: Protein sequence of P57 (derived from gene sequence)

steps.description: Homologous sequences to P57 were searched using the BLAST against NCBI nr database and the MEROPS database 3 (Rawlings et al., 2006) with default parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: search for protein homologous sequences using BLASTP against NCBI nr and MEROPS

steps.description: The representative homologs to P57 were aligned by using DNAMAN software.
steps.searchable_summary: align representative protein homologous sequences

steps.description: The molecular mass of mature P57 was determined to be 46,016 Da by MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry.
steps.searchable_summary: determine protein molecular mass by MALDI-TOF MS

steps.description: SignalP 3.0 (Bendtsen et al., 2004) prediction suggested that the pre-sequence of P57 contains an N-terminal signal peptide sequence (Met1-Ala29).
steps.searchable_summary: predict protein signal peptide using SignalP

steps.description: According to the result of N-terminal sequencing, the first N-terminal residue of mature P57 is Ser124. ... Thus, based on the sequence and the molecular mass of P57, it was predicted that mature P57 contains 442 residues from Ser124 to Ala565.
steps.searchable_summary: predict mature protein sequence based on N-terminal sequencing and molecular mass
inputs: Full protein sequence of P57 precursor
N-terminal amino acid sequence of mature P57

steps.description: Multiple sequence alignment suggests that the catalytic triad of P57 is composed of Asp153, His188 and Ser492 ( Figure 3B).
steps.searchable_summary: identify catalytic triad residues based on multiple sequence alignment
inputs: Multiple protein sequence alignment of P57 and homologs

methods.description: Procedure to assess the binding ability of wild P57, its PA domain (as EGFP fusion), and PA domain mutants (as EGFP fusions) to bovine-insoluble type I collagen fiber using fluorescence measurement or SDS-PAGE.

steps.description: To analyze the binding ability of the PA domain and its mutants toward collagen, 5 mg collagen fibers were mixed with 500 µL PA-EGFP, EGFP or the mutants (0.5 mg/mL) in 50 mM Tris-HCl buffer (pH 8.0).
steps.searchable_summary: mix collagen fibers with protein/mutants in buffer

steps.description: The mixture was incubated at 37 • C for 2 h...
steps.searchable_summary: incubate mixture of collagen and protein

steps.description: ...and then centrifuged at 12,000 g and 4 • C for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge mixture to separate bound and unbound protein

steps.description: The free fluorescence intensity in the supernatant before and after incubation was measured on a FP-6500 spectrofluorometer (Jasco, Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: measure fluorescence intensity in supernatant

steps.description: Fraction of fusion protein bound (%) = (B-A)/B*100%, B and A represent the free fluorescence intensity in the solution before and after a fusion protein binds to collagen, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percentage of protein bound based on fluorescence intensity
outputs: Percentage of fusion protein bound to collagen

steps.description: The collagen-binding ability of wild P57 was assayed as described by Itoi et al. (2006). Purified P57 (0.2 mg/mL, 0.5 mL) in 20 mM Na 2 HPO 4 -NaH 2 PO 4 (pH 7.0) containing 10 mM Fe 3+ was incubated at 4 • C for 1 h to inhibit the enzyme activity.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate purified P57 with Fe3+ to inhibit activity

steps.description: Then collagen fibers (1, 5, or 10 mg) was added and the mixture was incubated at 37 • C for 2 h with stirring.
steps.searchable_summary: mix inhibited P57 with collagen fibers and incubate with stirring
inputs: Bovine-insoluble Type I collagen fibers (various amounts)
outputs: Mixture of inhibited P57 and collagen

steps.description: After incubation, the mixture was centrifuged for 10 min at 12,000 g and 4 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge mixture to separate bound and unbound protein
inputs: Mixture of inhibited P57 and collagen

steps.description: The supernatant was analyzed by 12.5% SDS-PAGE.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze supernatant by SDS-PAGE to detect unbound protein
outputs: SDS-PAGE gel image/data showing unbound P57

steps.description: BSA as a negative control was treated as P57.
steps.searchable_summary: perform negative control using BSA

methods.description: Experimental procedures to determine the biochemical properties of the purified protease P57, including molecular mass, optimal temperature, thermostability, optimal pH, effect of NaCl, effect of metal ions and inhibitors, and substrate specificity.

steps.description: SDS-PAGE analysis showed that the purified protease had high purity, with an apparent molecular mass of approximately 45 kDa (Figure 2A).
steps.searchable_summary: determine apparent protein molecular mass by SDS-PAGE

steps.description: With casein as substrate, the optimum temperature of P57 was 40 • C, and 10% of the maximum activity was retained at 0 • C ( Figure 2B). Enzyme activity was measured at 0-70 • C in 20 mM phosphate buffer (pH 7.0).
steps.searchable_summary: measure enzyme activity at different temperatures to determine optimum temperature

steps.description: Thermostability of P57. The enzyme was incubated at 30 • C, 40 • C, or 45 • C for different time periods, and then residual activity was measured at 40 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: measure enzyme residual activity after incubation at different temperatures and times to assess thermostability
inputs: Assay buffer (20 mM phosphate buffer pH 7.0)
outputs: Enzyme residual activity vs. time/temperature data

steps.description: Effect of pH on the activity of P57. Enzyme activity was measured at 40 • C in different pH buffers (20 mM): Na 2 HPO 4 -citric acid (pH 4.0-6.0), Na 2 HPO 4 -NaH 2 PO 4 (pH 6.0-8.0), Tris-HCl (pH 8.0-10.0), and Na 2 CO 3 -NaHCO 3 (pH 9.0-11.0).
steps.searchable_summary: measure enzyme activity at different pH values to determine optimum pH

steps.description: Effect of NaCl on the activity of P57. Enzyme activity was measured at 40 • C in different concentrations of NaCl.
steps.searchable_summary: measure enzyme activity at different NaCl concentrations
outputs: Enzyme activity vs. NaCl concentration data

steps.description: Effects of metal ions and inhibitors on P57 activity were shown in Table 2. P57 activity was not affected by Li + , K + , Ba 2+ , Co 2+ , or Sr 2+ . At the final concentration of 8 mM, Cu 2+ , Zn 2+ , and Ni 2+ obviously inhibited P57 activity, and Fe 3+ severely inhibited P57 activity by 96.4%. While Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ slightly activated the enzyme activity, Mn 2+ significantly increased P57 activity to 194.1%, showing an activating effect on P57 activity. In addition, P57 activity was strongly inhibited by PMSF, a serine protease inhibitor, indicating that P57 is likely a serine protease. The activity of P57 was also inhibited by metal chelators EDTA and EGTA, but not affected by o-P or IA.
steps.searchable_summary: measure enzyme activity in the presence of various metal ions and inhibitors
inputs: Various metal ions (Li+, K+, Ba2+, Co2+, Sr2+, Cu2+, Zn2+, Ni2+, Fe3+, Ca2+, Mg2+, Mn2+)
Various inhibitors (PMSF, EDTA, EGTA, o-P, IA)
outputs: Enzyme activity data in presence of metal ions/inhibitors
Effect of metal ions/inhibitors on activity

steps.description: Substrate specificity analysis showed that P57 could hydrolyze casein, gelatin and collagen, with the highest activity toward gelatin, the degenerated form of collagen (Table 3). P57 showed no activity toward elastin. Among the synthetic peptides, P57 had higher activities toward AAPL and AAPF and lower activities toward FAAF, AAPR, AAPK and AAVA (Table 3).
steps.searchable_summary: measure enzyme activity using various substrates to determine substrate specificity
inputs: Various substrates (casein, gelatin, collagen, elastin, synthetic peptides AAPL, AAPF, FAAF, AAPR, AAPK, AAVA)
outputs: Enzyme activity data for various substrates
